mission.title: Install and Configure Redpanda
mission.description: Redpanda is a high-performance streaming data platform that is fully compatible with the Kafka API, designed to be faster and simpler to operate without the need for ZooKeeper or JVM. It is ideal for developers looking for a lightweight solution for real-time data streaming. Key trade-offs include the absence of some Kafka ecosystem features due to its design choices.

steps.title: Add the Redpanda Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Redpanda Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add redpanda https://charts.redpanda.com

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for Redpanda Helm charts.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Redpanda CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Redpanda to function correctly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/v25.3.10/deploy/crds/redpanda.vectorized.io_redpandas.yaml

```

> This command installs the Redpanda CRDs.

steps.title: Install Redpanda using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Redpanda in the specified namespace using the Helm chart.

```bash

helm install redpanda redpanda/redpanda --namespace redpanda --create-namespace --version 25.3.10

```

> This installs Redpanda in the 'redpanda' namespace.

steps.title: Create a Redpanda Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource for the Redpanda cluster to define its configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vectorized.io/v1beta1
kind: Redpanda
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: redpanda
spec:
  replicas: 3
  kafka:
    storage:
      volumeClaimTemplate:
        spec:
          resources:
            requests:
              storage: 10Gi
EOF

```

> This creates a Redpanda cluster with 3 replicas and 10Gi of storage.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vectorized.io/v1beta1
kind: Redpanda
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: redpanda
spec:
  replicas: 3
  kafka:
    storage:
      volumeClaimTemplate:
        spec:
          resources:
            requests:
              storage: 10Gi
EOF

steps.title: Verify Redpanda Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Redpanda cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get redpandas -n redpanda

```

> This command lists the Redpanda clusters in the 'redpanda' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Redpanda
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Redpanda installation and its associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall redpanda --namespace redpanda

kubectl delete namespace redpanda

```

> This command uninstalls the Redpanda release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Redpanda
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will drain and cordon the nodes if necessary, upgrade the Helm release, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain my-cluster-0 --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade redpanda redpanda/redpanda --namespace redpanda --version 25.3.10

kubectl uncordon my-cluster-0

kubectl get redpandas -n redpanda

```

> Drains the first node of the Redpanda cluster.
> Upgrades the Redpanda installation to the specified version.
> Uncordons the node to allow scheduling.
> Verifies the status of the Redpanda cluster after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Redpanda'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Redpanda pods are not running or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n redpanda' and ensure all pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient storage for Redpanda'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the persistent volume claims are not being fulfilled.

**Fix:**
Ensure your storage class is correctly configured and has available capacity.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Redpanda pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen due to misconfiguration or resource limits being exceeded.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n redpanda' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Redpanda is not reachable from client'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the service is not correctly exposed or if there are network policies blocking access.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration with 'kubectl get svc -n redpanda' and check network policies.

versionNotes.changes: Improved performance for high-throughput workloads, added support for tiered storage.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Redpanda cluster listed with the command 'kubectl get redpandas -n redpanda', showing the status as 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources.
